answer:
George’s expected utility from betting must be at least his utility from not betting. With logarithmic utility, the condition is \( 0.5 \ln(1,100,000) + 0.5 \ln(900,000) \) for even money, but here the probability is unknown. Solving \( p \ln(1,100,000) + (1-p) \ln(900,000) \geq \ln(1,000,000) \) gives \( p \geq \frac{\ln(1,000,000) - \ln(900,000)}{\ln(1,100,000) - \ln(900,000)} \approx 0.525 \).  

\boxed{A}
